trigger.summary: HRV dropped from 76 ms baseline to 61 ms average with lows near 50 ms.

vitality_ranked_candidates.mechanism: HRV-guided training uses autonomic status to modulate load, reducing mismatch between sympathetic stress and training intensity. This protects an elite HRV axis without simply saying maintain.
vitality_ranked_candidates.intervention: For 6 weeks, if morning/wearable HRV is >10 ms below personal baseline for 2 consecutive days or resting HR is elevated with poor sleep, replace that day’s intensity with zone-2 only plus mobility; resume intensity after 2 recovered days.

n_of_1.measurable_signal: 7-day HRV average, number of HRV dip days, next-day RPE
n_of_1.decision_criterion: continue if HRV returns to within 5 ms of 76 ms baseline and dip days fall below 2/week; iterate if HRV improves 0-5 ms; drop if conditioning or Life score worsens without HRV recovery

spareq.s_reason: HRV dip is load-bearing for vitality but not an acute medical severity signal.
spareq.p_reason: Directly matched to documented HRV perturbation and elite training phenotype.
spareq.a_reason: Direct wearable HRV signal with a validated HRV-guided training N-of-1 style loop.
spareq.r_reason: No new equipment; only changes training-day decision rules.
spareq.e_reason: Reinforces risk model by avoiding overtraining and injury; no conflict with aspirin, alcohol minimization, or radiation stewardship.
spareq.q_reason: Supported by RCT-level HRV-guided endurance training literature.

vitality_ranked_candidates.risk_interaction: reinforces:reduces overtraining/injury exposure and does not contradict active risk adjuncts

vitality_ranked_candidates.biomcp_query: HRV biofeedback randomized trial meta analysis

trigger.summary: Autonomic recovery is deflecting downward despite high baseline HRV.

vitality_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Slow paced breathing near resonance frequency increases baroreflex engagement and vagal modulation, plausibly improving HRV and stress recovery.
vitality_ranked_candidates.intervention: 10 minutes daily of paced breathing at approximately 5.5-6 breaths/min, ideally same time each day, using HRV biofeedback if available.

n_of_1.decision_criterion: continue if 7-day HRV rises by at least 5 ms or returns above 70 ms by week 4; iterate timing/dose if 0-5 ms improvement; drop if HRV declines or adherence below 70%

spareq.p_reason: Good match for autonomic recovery, though not specific to this patient’s exact dip cause.
spareq.a_reason: HRV is directly measurable inside the 4-week trial window.
spareq.r_reason: Low burden and no special equipment required beyond app/timer.
spareq.e_reason: No conflict with risk adjuncts; may support stress regulation.
spareq.q_reason: Meta-analytic support for HRV biofeedback effects.

vitality_ranked_candidates.risk_interaction: none:no contradiction with aspirin, alcohol minimization, H. pylori testing, radiation stewardship, or resistant starch

trigger.summary: Sleep is generally adequate but has 2-3 sub-5-hour nights/month.

vitality_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Sleep extension before predictable disruption can reduce accumulated sleep debt and improve performance, mood, and recovery signals.
vitality_ranked_candidates.intervention: For any known late-work, travel, or social night, schedule two prior nights with an 8.0-8.5 hour sleep opportunity by moving bedtime earlier, not wake time later.

n_of_1.measurable_signal: number of sub-5-hour nights/month and average total sleep time
n_of_1.decision_criterion: continue if sub-5-hour nights fall from 2-3/month to 0-1/month; iterate if unchanged but average sleep rises; drop if adherence below 50%

spareq.s_reason: Sub-5-hour nights are a discrete disruption pocket in an otherwise strong sleep profile.
spareq.p_reason: Directly targets the documented failure mode rather than general sleep advice.
spareq.a_reason: Wearable total sleep and sub-5-hour frequency are measurable within weeks.
spareq.e_reason: Reinforces risk model by reducing sleep-loss driven alcohol/craving/training-error risk.
spareq.q_reason: Sleep-extension trials support performance and recovery benefits, though athlete analog extrapolation applies.

vitality_ranked_candidates.risk_interaction: reinforces:better sleep supports cardiometabolic and behavioral risk goals

trigger.summary: BJJ loss narrowed the social-physical recovery channel.

vitality_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Group exercise can improve perceived stress, mental quality of life, and social reinforcement beyond solitary training. This replaces part of BJJ’s social channel without needing live grappling.
vitality_ranked_candidates.intervention: Book one recurring weekly gym class or partner session for 6 weeks with the same people/time; intensity can be moderate, but the social appointment is the active ingredient.

n_of_1.measurable_signal: weekly social-physical sessions completed and subjective Life score
n_of_1.decision_criterion: continue if at least 5 of 6 sessions are completed and Life score rises by ≥5 points; iterate group/time if adherence succeeds but Life does not improve; drop if adherence <50%

spareq.s_reason: Social-physical narrowing is load-bearing for current vitality.
spareq.p_reason: Direct replacement for the lost BJJ social channel using existing gym context.
spareq.a_reason: Session completion and weekly Life score are measurable during the trial.
spareq.e_reason: No conflict with risk adjuncts; may reduce need for alcohol-centered socializing.
spareq.q_reason: RCT evidence supports group exercise effects on stress and quality of life.

vitality_ranked_candidates.risk_interaction: reinforces:creates non-alcohol social recovery consistent with alcohol minimization

trigger.summary: REM/deep sleep appear vulnerable during disruption pockets.

vitality_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Evening short-wavelength light suppresses melatonin and can delay sleep timing; blue-light blocking may improve sleep continuity in susceptible individuals.
vitality_ranked_candidates.intervention: For 3 weeks, wear blue-blocking glasses or use strict blue-light reduction for the final 2 hours before intended sleep on weeknights.

n_of_1.measurable_signal: sleep efficiency, REM minutes, and sleep onset latency if available
n_of_1.decision_criterion: continue if sleep efficiency improves by ≥3 percentage points or REM rises by ≥15 minutes without later bedtime; iterate if only onset improves; drop if no signal changes

spareq.s_reason: Sleep is not globally poor but has architecture/disruption vulnerability.
spareq.p_reason: Most useful if evening light exposure is present; baseline exposure is unknown.
spareq.a_reason: Wearable sleep efficiency and REM are measurable within weeks.
spareq.e_reason: No conflict with active risk adjuncts.
spareq.q_reason: Single-RCT support, strongest in insomnia/sleep-delay analog populations.

vitality_ranked_candidates.risk_interaction: none:no contradiction with active risk adjuncts

trigger.summary: BJJ previously supplied high-intensity discharge, social contact, and skill novelty.

vitality_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Bag or pad-based striking intervals can approximate BJJ’s sympathetic discharge and skill-learning components while avoiding live grappling. Non-sparring format limits head-impact and bleeding risk.
vitality_ranked_candidates.intervention: Try one coached boxing/kickboxing bag or pad class weekly for 6 weeks, explicitly no sparring and no head contact.

n_of_1.measurable_signal: post-session Life score, next-morning HRV, and session RPE
n_of_1.decision_criterion: continue if Life rises by ≥5 points and next-morning HRV is not >10 ms below baseline after sessions; iterate intensity if Life improves but HRV drops; drop if HRV repeatedly drops or injury/bruising occurs

spareq.s_reason: Targets a lost high-intensity recovery valve.
spareq.p_reason: Functional match to BJJ discharge and novelty, though not identical social grappling.
spareq.a_reason: Life, RPE, and next-morning HRV are measurable in the trial window.
spareq.r_reason: Requires finding an appropriate no-sparring class.
spareq.e_reason: Aspirin chemoprevention makes bruising/trauma relevant; no-sparring constraint mitigates but does not erase this.
spareq.q_reason: Evidence is extrapolated from HIIT plus N-of-1 valve-function matching.

vitality_ranked_candidates.risk_interaction: reinforces:acceptable only with no sparring/head contact because aspirin increases bleeding/bruising concern

trigger.summary: VO2max is elite and should be protected with a protocol, not maintenance advice.

vitality_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Polarized training separates low-intensity volume from limited high-intensity work, reducing gray-zone load that can erode recovery while preserving aerobic capacity.
vitality_ranked_candidates.intervention: For 8 weeks, cap training at approximately 80% zone 1-2 and 20% zone 4-5 by time, with no more than two high-intensity sessions/week.

n_of_1.measurable_signal: zone-time distribution, RPE drift, and 7-day HRV average
n_of_1.decision_criterion: continue if RPE drift falls and HRV remains within 5 ms of baseline while high-intensity capacity is preserved; iterate if HRV improves but performance feels flat; drop if motivation or performance declines

spareq.s_reason: Elite axis protection rather than current severe deflection.
spareq.p_reason: Good match for high-performing endurance/mixed-sport profile.
spareq.a_reason: Zone time, RPE, and HRV are trackable weekly.
spareq.r_reason: Requires discipline and accurate zone tracking.
spareq.e_reason: Compatible with cardiovascular risk profile and avoids excess strain.
spareq.q_reason: RCT evidence supports polarized training benefits versus other distributions.

vitality_ranked_candidates.risk_interaction: reinforces:supports cardiometabolic fitness without increasing risk-adjunct conflict

vitality_ranked_candidates.action_name: Low-dose melatonin rescue for high-risk short-sleep nights

trigger.summary: Occasional short-sleep nights may be preventable when sleep onset is the limiting step.

vitality_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Melatonin can shift circadian timing and modestly reduce sleep onset latency, especially when timed before intended bedtime.
vitality_ranked_candidates.intervention: On planned high-risk short-sleep nights only, trial melatonin 0.3-1 mg 60-90 minutes before target bedtime; do not combine with alcohol.

n_of_1.measurable_signal: sleep onset latency, total sleep time, and next-day grogginess
n_of_1.decision_criterion: continue only for rescue use if sleep onset improves by ≥15 minutes and no next-day grogginess occurs; iterate dose downward if groggy; drop if no onset benefit

spareq.s_reason: Targets intermittent sleep disruption, not chronic insomnia.
spareq.p_reason: Best fit if short nights are caused by delayed sleep onset.
spareq.a_reason: Sleep onset and total sleep are measurable within two weeks.
spareq.r_reason: Simple but requires careful timing and avoidance of routine use creep.
spareq.e_reason: No direct conflict, but must not be paired with alcohol; supplement use should be conservative with eGFR watch.

vitality_ranked_candidates.risk_interaction: reinforces:explicitly avoids alcohol co-use and may reduce short-sleep recovery stress

trigger.summary: Subjective Life score is below personal baseline despite high condition score.

vitality_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Brief positive-psychology exercises can increase attention to rewarding events and perceived meaning, improving subjective wellbeing in some people.
vitality_ranked_candidates.intervention: For 14 nights, write three specific good things from the day and one sentence on why each happened.

n_of_1.decision_criterion: continue if Life score rises by ≥5 points by day 14; iterate to weekly cadence if benefit occurs but daily burden is annoying; drop if no Life-score change

spareq.s_reason: Life 75 is below baseline but not severely impaired.
spareq.p_reason: Directly targets subjective wellbeing rather than sleep or fitness.
spareq.a_reason: Primary signal is subjective Life score only.
spareq.r_reason: Very low cost and low time burden.
spareq.q_reason: RCT evidence exists for positive-psychology exercises, though response is individual.

vitality_ranked_candidates.risk_interaction: none:no contradiction with active risk adjuncts
